6th March, 2005 · Turning 21 · Dhaka, Bangladesh

Shefa

Twenty-one years of being the kind of person
the world quietly depends on.

Her story begins here ↓

✦ The Beginning ✦

On 6th March, 2005, when Dhaka was experiencing one of its coolest early springs — a baby arrived in this world, apparently to contribute to global warming. 🌡️

Twenty-one years later, the world is genuinely warmer because of her. Not the climate — the people in it.

Her name means healing. She never had to try to live up to it. She just did.

🌸
✦
Identity I

A Daughter

Before she was anything else to the world, she was someone's entire world first. Her parents' trust in her is not blind — it is earned, daily, in a hundred small and unremarkable ways that add up to something remarkable.

There is something particular about how she loves her family — it is not loud or performative. It is the kind that shows up without being asked, notices without being told, and stays without needing a reason.

"Her parents didn't raise a daughter. They raised someone the whole family quietly leans on — without ever making it feel like a burden."
👨‍👩‍👧 A photo with family Upload to add here
🌿
👯 A photo with siblings Upload to add here
Identity II

A Sister

The oldest. Which means she was the first to figure everything out — so her siblings wouldn't have to make the same mistakes alone. She's the one dragging Aman to the gym at night, and then both of them ending up lying on the floor being dramatic about life together.

That is exactly what a great eldest sibling looks like: equal parts responsible and ridiculous. A safe space disguised as a person.

"She didn't just grow up first. She made sure growing up looked less scary for everyone who came after her."
❀
✿
Identity III

A Friend

She asks "did you eat?" at 6:30 in the evening — not because she has to, but because she already had a feeling you hadn't. And she was right. She is usually right.

She is the kind of friend who notices what you don't say. Who shows up in the small gaps between your words. Who makes a 5-hour BoD meeting survivable just by texting back.

"She doesn't just listen to what you say. She listens to the shape of what you leave out — and meets you there."
🤝 A photo with friends Upload to add here
🌹
✦
🌹 A photo for this chapter Upload to add here
Identity IV

A Partner

She is a hopeless romantic who has read enough Dostoevsky to know exactly how badly love can go — and chooses to believe in it anyway. That is not naivety. That is courage.

She brings warmth without losing herself, honesty without cruelty, and a rare ability to make someone feel genuinely chosen — not convenient. The kind of person who makes you want to be worth it.

"She cried over White Nights and still believes in love. If that isn't the most Shefa thing — brave, feeling, and completely unashamed of it."
🐾
Identity V

A Cat Lover

She doesn't own a cat yet. But every cat on her campus knows her. Every neighbourhood stray has been looked at with that specific expression — the one that says "you could come home with me if you wanted."

There is something telling about the way she loves what she cannot yet keep. She gives it freely anyway — no ownership required. That is not a small thing. That is a very specific kind of generous.

"She said she can handle any pet except cockroaches. The cats in her life don't know how lucky they are — yet. One day they will."
🌷
✦
Identity VI

A Kind Human Being

She runs into the rain and skips home laughing. She reads Crime and Punishment and follows it up with whatever nonsense she finds next, unapologetically. She discusses the tragedy of Nastenka with the same intensity she uses to debate which BPL team deserves her loyalty.

She is a full, whole, wonderfully contradictory human being — and her kindness is not in spite of that complexity. It is because of it. She has felt enough to understand. And understanding made her gentle.

"She dreams of disappearing to a quiet valley with no signal, just tea and boiled potatoes. And she would make that sound like the most beautiful thing in the world — because when she says it, it is."
🌿 A candid photo Upload to add here

Twenty Years, Briefly

✦ A handful of moments ✦

2005
Arrives in Dhaka on a cool March morning
Growing up
Discovers books. Falls in love with words.
Somewhere
Reads Dostoevsky. Cries. Reads him again.
Always
Runs into the rain. Skips home laughing.
Still waiting
Adopts every campus cat with her eyes.
2026
Turns twenty-one. Still the most radiant person in the room.

Happy Birthday

"Twenty-one years of healing the people around you
without ever being asked.

Of running into the rain, loving Russian tragedies,
caring too much, feeling everything fully,
and being entirely, stubbornly yourself.

Your name means healing, Shefa.
You have always, quietly, lived up to it."
"You asked for a place where you can just rest —
where the noise stops and someone simply holds the ground.

You don't ever have to look for it again.
No matter how chaotic the world gets,
no matter how heavy it all feels —

this safe space is yours.

Happy Birthday, Shefa."

✦ 6th March, 2026 ✦
